statement.informal: Division of two positive reals is computable in 2 F16 nodes via the chain D_F16(x, D_F13(−1, y)). The inner D_F13(−1, y) computes y⁻¹; the outer D_F16 computes x · y⁻¹ = x/y. (with constant 1 promoted to a parameter k8)
statement.formal_lean:
theorem div_two_node_pos_domain (k8 : ℕ) (x y : ℝ) (hx : 0 < x) (hy : 0 < y) :
    D_Fk86 x (D_F13 (-1) y) = x / y

tactics:
theorem div_two_node_pos_domain (x y : ℝ) (hx : 0 < x) (hy : 0 < y) :
    D_F16 x (D_F13 (-1) y) = x / y := by
  have hinv : D_F13 (-1 : ℝ) y = y⁻¹ := by
    unfold D_F13
    rw [show (-1 : ℝ) * Real.log y = -(Real.log y) from by ring]
    rw [Real.exp_neg, Real.exp_log hy]
  rw [hinv]
  unfold D_F16
  rw [show Real.log y⁻¹ = -(Real.log y) from Real.log_inv y]
  rw [Real.exp_add, Real.exp_log hx, Real.exp_neg, Real.exp_log hy]
  field_simp

metadata.verification_pending: Phase 1.5 — port legacy theorems to MachLib foundations before kernel re-verification.
